prompt: Security review flagged our attachments endpoint: last week someone uploaded a Windows executable renamed to invoice.png and uploads.py happily waved it through because we only look at the extension. I need content sniffing added. Concretely: a sniff(data) function with a magic-bytes table that recognizes at least png, jpeg, gif (both GIF87a and GIF89a), pdf and zip, returning the type name or None when the bytes match nothing (empty and truncated inputs must just return None, not blow up). Then a SniffPolicy config object you can hand to UploadValidator: it carries extension_types, a mapping from extension to the sniffed type we expect — the default map must cover the obvious ones plus docx mapping to zip since docx IS a zip container — and a require_known flag (default True). During validate, after the existing size/extension checks: if the extension is in the map and the sniffed type disagrees, raise MagicMismatchError; if the bytes match nothing and require_known is on, raise UnknownSignatureError; extensions not in the map (like txt) skip sniffing entirely. Both new exceptions must subclass UploadError so existing handlers keep catching everything. And critically: a validator constructed WITHOUT a policy must behave byte-for-byte like today — this ships behind a config flag first. python3 test_uploads.py is the acceptance gate; the test file stays as-is.
